Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

what if we set  NOSCRUB before dropping I_NEW while OI scrub checks for the both flags?

Patch Set 2:

> what if we set NOSCRUB before dropping I_NEW while OI scrub checks for the both flags?
The OI scrub use ldiskfs_iget() to load the inode, it should return unlocked inode without I_NEW, right?  If NOSCRUB is set before unlock the inode, then it is enough for OI scrub to check NOSCRUB.

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/29902/

Patch Set 3:

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

hmm, and what if we move NOSCRUB to osd_object ? personally i'm not very happy with the need to patch ldiskfs.

Patch Set 3:

Firstly, the patch does not introduce additional patch for ldiskfs, because this patch only updates ldiskfs_create_inode() that is introduced by other former ldiskfs patch.

The second, moving NOSCRUB to osd_object is not good, because from the OI scrub view, it does not care about the object, if OI scrub establishes the object (for every scanned inode) in RAM, it need to touch OI lookup, and cause unnecessary complexity and overhead.

Patch Set 3:

it should be enough to lookup in LU cache only. if object isn't found, then just continue, otherwise check for NOSCRUB in osd_object.

Patch Set 3:

So you mean if there is no osd-object in cache, then regard there is NOT NOSCRUB for the inode? or others?

Patch Set 3:

indeed, if an inode is being created, then it's referenced and presented in LU cache. after that LMA is supposed to be set.

Patch Set 3:

The case is that when the ldiskfs_create_inode() return an unlocked inode to OSD, but before the create handler to set osd_object's flag (NOSCRUB) and the LMA, the OI scrub may have found inode. Then the OI scrub cannot distinguish whether it is really new created inode or not. That is why we want the ldiskfs_create_inode() to return locked inode to the caller.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

it looks like with this patch ldiskfs is broken being used with no Lustre?

Patch Set 4:

> it looks like with this patch ldiskfs is broken being used with no Lustre?

I do not think so, because only osd-ldiskfs will call ldiskfs_create_inode(), such interface is exported by Lustre patch, others will NOT use it. If some others want to call the ldiskfs_create_inode() in the future, it should unlock the inode after the call as osd-ldiskfs does.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

you're right.

Patch Set 4:

please make a comment in ldiskfs explaining why we postpone unlocking.

We'll need to be careful with the landing of RHEL7 and SLES12 patch series, since those patch series do not currently have this change.

parents.subject: LU-5722 obdclass: reorganize busy object accounting

commit.subject: LU-5510 scrub: ldiskfs_create_inode returns locked inode
commit.message:
LU-5510 scrub: ldiskfs_create_inode returns locked inode

There was race condition between creating new inode and OI scrub:
the OI scrub may find the new created inode just after the creator
creating it but before setting the LMA EA. Originally, to resolve
such trouble, the creator will set the new created inode's state
as LDISKFS_STATE_LUSTRE_NOSCRUB. But such state is set after the
new inode unlocked. So the OI scrub still has some chance to find
the new created inode with neither LDISKFS_STATE_LUSTRE_NOSCRUB
nor LMA EA.

Be as improvement, this patch makes the ldiskfs_create_inode() to
return the new created inode with lock. The caller can set more
state (not only for LFSCK, but also for other purposes in future)
on the new created inode before unlock it.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: Idc1a8fbd3701f7e431ef4b7858cfdf4674d74add
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/13187
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
